questions.q: Who presided over the 1929 Lahore session of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru presided over the 1929 Lahore session of the Congress.

questions.q: What major resolution was adopted at the 1929 Lahore session?
questions.explanation: The Lahore session adopted the resolution for Purna Swaraj, or complete independence.

questions.q: On the bank of which river was the tricolour hoisted during the Lahore session?
questions.explanation: The tricolour was hoisted on the bank of the river Ravi at Lahore.

questions.q: In which year was this historic Congress session held at Lahore?
questions.explanation: The session that declared complete independence was held at Lahore in 1929.

questions.q: The Lahore session called for the first observance of Independence Day on which date?
questions.explanation: The session resolved that 26 January 1930 would be observed as Independence Day.

questions.q: At the time of the 1929 session, Lahore was the capital of which province?
questions.explanation: Lahore was the capital of the undivided Punjab province at the time.

questions.q: The Lahore session's declaration of complete independence is often called by which name?
questions.explanation: The declaration of complete independence is known as the Purna Swaraj resolution.

questions.q: The Lahore session set the stage for which mass movement that began in 1930?
questions.explanation: The Lahore session led directly into the Civil Disobedience Movement of 1930.

questions.q: Jawaharlal Nehru, the session's president, was the son of which earlier Congress leader?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the son of Motilal Nehru, himself a former Congress president.

questions.q: The flag hoisted at Lahore symbolised the pledge of what?
questions.explanation: The flag-hoisting on the Ravi symbolised the pledge of Purna Swaraj, complete independence.

questions.q: In which month of 1929 was the Lahore session held?
questions.explanation: The Lahore session was held in December 1929, with the flag hoisted at midnight on 31 December.

questions.q: Whose insistence largely ensured that the younger Nehru was made president at Lahore?
questions.explanation: Gandhi backed Jawaharlal Nehru for the presidency at Lahore, helping elevate the younger leader.

questions.q: The Lahore session followed the failure of the British to grant which status promised in the Nehru Report?
questions.explanation: When Dominion status sought in the Nehru Report was not granted, the Congress moved to demand full independence.

questions.q: Which Viceroy's vague 'Dominion' declaration shortly before the session failed to satisfy the Congress?
questions.explanation: Lord Irwin's October 1929 statement on eventual Dominion status was too vague, hardening the Congress stand at Lahore.

questions.q: At Lahore, the Congress fixed the last Sunday of January for celebrating Independence Day, which fell on what date in 1930?
questions.explanation: The last Sunday of January 1930 fell on 26 January, which became Independence Day.

questions.q: The Lahore session authorised the launch of which form of resistance against unjust laws?
questions.explanation: The session sanctioned a programme of civil disobedience to follow the independence declaration.

questions.q: Jawaharlal Nehru at Lahore was among the youngest leaders ever to head the Congress, taking over from whom as president?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru succeeded his father Motilal Nehru, who had presided over the previous Calcutta session.

questions.q: The Lahore session is sometimes linked to the city's atmosphere of revolutionary fervour, heightened by the trial of whom at the time?
questions.explanation: The Lahore Conspiracy Case against Bhagat Singh and others was underway, lending the city a charged mood.

questions.q: The flag-hoisting at the Lahore session took place at what symbolic moment?
questions.explanation: Nehru hoisted the tricolour at the stroke of midnight as the New Year of 1930 began.

questions.q: Which slogan best captured the goal proclaimed at the Lahore session?
questions.explanation: The session's central goal was Purna Swaraj, complete independence, proclaimed with cries of its support.

questions.q: The Lahore session resolution committed the Congress to withdraw from which bodies as part of its programme?
questions.explanation: The resolution called on Congress members to withdraw from the legislatures as part of the new struggle.

questions.q: On which exact date was the Purna Swaraj resolution passed at the Lahore session?
questions.explanation: The resolution was passed on 19 December 1929; the flag was hoisted at midnight on 31 December.

questions.q: The 1929 session was the second time the Congress met at Lahore; in what broad period had it first met there?
questions.explanation: The Congress had earlier held its 1893 session at Lahore, making 1929 a return to the city.

questions.q: Just before the Lahore session, Gandhi and other leaders met Viceroy Irwin in a conference that failed in which month of 1929?
questions.explanation: A meeting with Irwin in December 1929 failed to yield a firm commitment, pushing the Congress to declare Purna Swaraj.

questions.q: The Lahore session resolution declared that the Congress would now treat which goal as having replaced Dominion status?
questions.explanation: The resolution formally replaced the older aim of Dominion status with complete independence.

questions.q: Which senior leader, father of the session's president, had chaired the constitutional committee whose report preceded the Lahore declaration?
questions.explanation: Motilal Nehru chaired the committee that produced the 1928 Nehru Report, which preceded the Lahore declaration.

questions.q: The Lahore session entrusted the conduct of the coming struggle largely to which leader's discretion?
questions.explanation: The session left the timing and form of civil disobedience largely to Gandhi's leadership.

questions.q: Radical leaders like Bose and others at Lahore had also pushed unsuccessfully for what additional step?
options: Setting up a parallel government / more radical action
questions.explanation: Left-wing leaders wanted more radical commitments, but the session's main outcome remained the Purna Swaraj declaration.

questions.q: Lahore, the venue of the 1929 session, became part of which country after the 1947 Partition?
questions.explanation: At Partition in 1947, Lahore became part of Pakistan.

questions.q: The independence pledge arising from the Lahore session was first publicly read out across India on which day?
questions.explanation: The Independence Day pledge was read out publicly across India on 26 January 1930.
